title: A New Dawn
blocks: The morning sun cast a golden hue heralding another day. Despite the
occasional wake-up call from dreams that reminded me of the moment my
belongings were lost, the morning scene here seemed as peaceful as ever.
My duties at the hostel -- helping with cooking, maintenance, and
assisting Daniel - were more than mere responsibilities. They were a
stabilizing force during this chaotic time, and helped me find solace in
this new "home." With its ambient hum of conversations and shared
stories, the hostel became a haven for my weary heart. Each sound, be it
laughter or a comforting word, was a testament to the empathy
surrounding me. Mariana and Rosa's kindness still echoed, and their

During a quiet moment in the afternoon, a concerned man named Eben from Norway approached me and listened to my story with genuine interest. He seemed to understand me more deeply than anyone had, his words warm with encouragement. Then, gently, he pressed a 100-peso bill into my hand. Although I refused several times, in the end I accepted it, moved by his kindness. Later that evening, a man named Masa from Japan gave me a thoughtful gift -- a notebook and pen. He explained that these tools would be helpful for documenting my travels in the future.
That evening, as I penned down my thoughts in this diary gifted by Masa,
I realized my journey had taken a transformative turn.
